lines.text: Hmm, these invasive plants are everywhere...
I should gather some native plant materials to help my friends.
I can tap to move around, and tap items to pick them up!
Did you know? Florida scrub jays like me are found ONLY in Florida. We're the state's only endemic bird species!
Oh dear, oh dear... my burrow entrance is completely blocked!
Those invasive Brazilian pepper plants grew right over it.
If only I had some nice saw palmetto fronds to rebuild...
Did you know? We gopher tortoises are a keystone species. Over 350 other animals use our burrows for shelter!
Are those... saw palmetto fronds?! Oh, thank you!
Now I can rebuild my burrow entrance properly.
Did you know? Saw palmetto is one of the most important native plants in Florida scrub. Its berries feed bears, deer, and birds!
Say, my friend Pip the mouse lives underground. Let me show you the way!
My burrow is looking great! Pip is down below if you need him.
Did you know? Gopher tortoise burrows can be up to 40 feet long and 10 feet deep. That's a lot of digging!
Hoo-hoo! Welcome to the Central Trail, young jay.
The preserve needs our help. Many of our animal friends are struggling.
Did you know? Lyonia Preserve protects over 400 acres of ancient Florida scrub — one of the rarest habitats on Earth!
Try visiting the Pine Clearing to the west, and the Sandy Barrens to the east.
Come back when you've helped everyone. I'll be watching from above!
Tap-tap-tap! Hey there, blue jay!
See that big sand pine? Those invasive air potato vines are strangling it!
I've been pecking at them all day but I can't keep up alone.
Can you help me cut them down? But careful - don't cut the native grape vines!
Did you know? Air potato vines can grow up to 8 inches per day! That's why they smother our native trees so quickly.
Tap-tap-TAP! The tree is breathing again, thanks to you!
Did you know? Sand pines depend on fire to release their seeds. Without natural fires, the scrub can't regenerate!
Here, take one of my feathers. It might come in handy!
EEK! Oh... oh, you scared me! I'm Pip!
I live down here in Shelly's burrow. It's nice and safe!
But I'm SO hungry... all my favorite scrub hickory nuts are gone.
The invasive plants crowded them out. Do you have any?
Did you know? Florida mice like me are one of many species that depend on gopher tortoise burrows for survival!
SCRUB HICKORY NUTS! Oh my oh my oh my!
*munch munch munch* These are PERFECT!
Did you know? Scrub hickory only grows in the sandy soils of Florida scrub. Each nut helps plant a new tree when we bury them!
Here, take this map I found. It shows where the rare plants grow!
*munch* Still working on these nuts! They're so good!
Did you know? By burying nuts and forgetting where we put them, mice like me accidentally plant new trees. We're nature's gardeners!
That map should help you find what you need. Good luck up there!
*yaaawn* Oh... hello there, little bird...
I'm so cold... all this overgrowth is blocking the sun from the sand.
I need open, warm sand to bask on. These invasive plants ruined my spot.
If only someone could plant some native ground cover to replace them...
Did you know? Sand skinks like me have tiny legs and 'swim' through loose sand. We need open, sunny patches to survive!
Ooh, is that a feather from Flicker? And rosemary cuttings too!
You've been busy helping the preserve! I trust you.
Did you know? Florida rosemary is a pioneer plant. It's one of the first to grow back after a natural fire clears the scrub!
Let's plant these native seeds from above. You fly, I'll guide you!
*stretches luxuriously* Ahhhh, warm sand at last!
The native plants are settling in nicely. Thank you, friend!
Did you know? Healthy Florida scrub needs periodic fires to stay open and sunny. Land managers use controlled burns to mimic natural fire cycles!
The path to the overlook is clear now. Sage is waiting for you up there!
Hoo-hoo! You've made it to the overlook, young jay.
I can see you've been busy helping our friends below.
But do you have proof of your restoration work?
Bring me the lyonia flowers, oak acorns, Flicker's feather, and Pip's map.
Remarkable! You truly have restored the preserve!
The lyonia flowers... the acorns... Flicker's feather... and Pip's map!
Did you know? Great horned owls like me are called the 'tigers of the sky.' We help keep the ecosystem balanced by controlling rodent populations!
Let's do one final survey together. The night watch begins!
The preserve is thriving once more, thanks to you.
You're a true friend of Lyonia Preserve, Scruff!
Did you know? Conservation efforts at places like Lyonia Preserve help protect endangered species and preserve Florida's unique natural heritage for future generations!
Magnificent! Every animal is safe in their restored habitat!
The native plants are flourishing, and the invasives are in retreat.
Did you know? You can visit real places like Lyonia Preserve in Deltona, Florida! Volunteers help remove invasive plants and protect native wildlife every day.
Thank you, Scruff. Lyonia Preserve will remember what you did today.
